channel.title: Morning Transportation
channel.description: Delivered every Monday by 10 a.m., Weekly Transportation examines the latest news in transportation and infrastructure politics and policy.

item.title: The reconciliation question
item.description: Will DOT chief Sean Duffy get a chance to snag more air traffic control funding in a new reconciliation bill?
item.dc:creator: By Sam Ogozalek and Chris Marquette
item.dc:contributor: By Sam Ogozalek and Chris Marquette

item.title: A busy transportation week ahead
item.description: Congress’ lengthy recess is over, with several transportation items on the agenda this week, including a vote on the ALERT Act.
item.dc:creator: By Sam Ogozalek and Oriana Pawlyk
item.dc:contributor: By Sam Ogozalek and Oriana Pawlyk

item.title: Budget szn
item.description: Here’s everything you need to know about how much money the White House wants for DOT in fiscal 2027 — and what it aims to do with it.
item.dc:creator: By Sam Ogozalek

item.title: Who will lead transportation panel after Graves’ exit?
item.description: With House Transportation Chair Sam Graves retiring, here are two key lawmakers aiming to take the committee’s top spot.
item.dc:creator: By Sam Ogozalek

item.title: A crucial week for TSA
item.description: As the DHS shutdown drags on, TSA’s acting leader will soon appear on Capitol Hill to detail the fallout. President Donald Trump is also deploying federal immigration agents to airports. And Elon Musk has entered the fray.
item.dc:creator: By Sam Ogozalek

item.title: Sununu’s shutdown forecast
item.description: We interviewed Airlines for America’s president and CEO about what it will take to resolve the ongoing DHS funding stalemate — and what its most drastic effects could be at airports.
item.dc:creator: By Sam Ogozalek

item.title: How Noem’s exit could help TSA chief
item.description: Could Noem’s departure from DHS be good news for TSA’s leader?
item.dc:creator: By Oriana Pawlyk

item.title: Hill fight over air safety enters new phase
item.description: House Transportation Chair Sam Graves (R-Mo.) recently told us he’s targeting this week to host a markup on his bipartisan ALERT Act.
item.dc:creator: By Sam Ogozalek

item.title: The ROTOR Act’s big week
item.description: The House is poised to vote on the ROTOR Act tomorrow. Will Transportation Chair Sam Graves oppose it?
item.dc:creator: By Sam Ogozalek, Chris Marquette and Oriana Pawlyk
item.dc:contributor: By Sam Ogozalek, Chris Marquette and Oriana Pawlyk
